# Twitter/X Launch Thread

> Post each section as a separate tweet in a thread. Include a screenshot or GIF with tweets 1, 3, and 5.

---

## Tweet 1 (Hook)

I built an open-source AI agent orchestration platform.

No Python. No notebooks. Just a visual UI.

It's called CrewForm — here's what it does 🧵👇

[Attach: dashboard screenshot]

## Tweet 2 (Problem)

Most AI agent frameworks require you to write Python.

That's great for developers — but not for the designer, marketer, or ops person on your team who also wants AI leverage.

CrewForm gives them a visual interface to create agents and assign tasks.

## Tweet 3 (The 3 Modes)

CrewForm has 3 ways to orchestrate agent teams:

🔗 Pipeline — chain agents sequentially (research → write → format)
🧠 Orchestrator — a brain agent delegates to specialists
💬 Collaboration — agents discuss and debate a topic together

All configurable through the UI.

[Attach: team mode screenshot]

## Tweet 4 (BYOK + Providers)

BYOK = Bring Your Own Key.

You connect your own API keys from any of 14 providers:
OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Groq, Mistral, Cohere, Perplexity, and more.

Zero markup. You pay your provider directly.

## Tweet 5 (Self-Hosting)

Self-hosting is one command:

```
docker compose up -d
```

PostgreSQL, frontend (nginx), task runner — all configured.

55+ database migrations run automatically.

Your data never leaves your server.

[Attach: terminal screenshot of docker compose]

## Tweet 6 (Integrations)

Integrations:
⚡ Zapier (7,000+ apps)
💬 Discord, Slack, Telegram, Email
📋 Trello, Asana
🔗 Webhooks + output routes

Trigger agents from anywhere. Deliver results anywhere.

## Tweet 7 (CTA)

CrewForm is open-source (AGPL-3.0).
